section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor finds the patient's blood pressure elevated.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Framed as a clinical interpretation of the vital sign (an assessment of being elevated) rather than simply documenting the measured value; this is part of the diagnostic impression.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's blood pressure is elevated.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a restatement of an objective vital sign status rather than a synthesized problem; it would more cleanly belong with observed findings, though its presence in A is not highly misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's heart function is reduced.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This sentence directly restates the echocardiogram finding (EF ~45%) and is essentially an objective test result summary; while interpretive, it is closely tied to imaging data and could reasonably be in O.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient has a history of congestive heart failure, depression, and hypertension.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Past medical history is typically documented in the subjective/history portion; its presence in A is common in brief notes but is more organizationally than clinically problematic.
